name: Iphigenia
description: Iphigenia, in Greek mythology, was the daughter of King Agamemnon and Queen Clytemnestra. Her most famous story involves her near-sacrifice at Aulis. The Greek fleet, preparing to sail to Troy for the Trojan War, was becalmed due to the anger of the goddess Artemis. The seer Calchas prophesied that the only way to appease Artemis and allow the fleet to sail was to sacrifice Iphigenia. Agamemnon initially resisted, but ultimately succumbed to pressure from the army and Odysseus. He lured Iphigenia to Aulis under the pretense of marrying Achilles. The events that followed vary in different versions of the myth. In some accounts, Iphigenia is sacrificed, accepting her fate to ensure the success of the Greek expedition. In other versions, Artemis intervenes at the last moment, whisking Iphigenia away to Tauris (present-day Crimea) and replacing her with a deer or a goat. In Tauris, Iphigenia becomes a priestess of Artemis, unknowingly performing human sacrifices on any Greeks who land there. She is eventually reunited with her brother, Orestes, and helps him steal a sacred statue of Artemis before they both return to Greece. Iphigenia represents themes of sacrifice, duty, divine intervention, and the complexities of family relationships during times of war. Her story highlights the difficult choices leaders face and the devastating consequences of divine wrath.
subtitle: Sacrifice, Duty, and Divine Intervention
achievements: Served as a priestess of Artemis
Helped Orestes steal the statue of Artemis

enemies.name: Thoas (King of Tauris, in some versions)

keyMyths.title: The Sacrifice at Aulis
keyMyths.description: To appease Artemis and allow the Greek fleet to sail to Troy, Iphigenia is lured to Aulis under the guise of marriage to Achilles and is nearly sacrificed. Different versions depict her death or miraculous escape and substitution.

keyMyths.title: Priestess in Tauris
keyMyths.description: In some versions, Artemis rescues Iphigenia and takes her to Tauris, where she becomes a priestess in Artemis's temple, unknowingly sacrificing Greeks. This isolation leads to a dramatic reunion with her brother.

keyMyths.title: Reunion with Orestes
keyMyths.description: Iphigenia is eventually reunited with her brother Orestes, who arrives in Tauris seeking to steal a sacred statue. Together, they devise a plan to escape Tauris and return to Greece.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A tragedy focusing on Iphigenia's sacrifice at Aulis.
sources.text: A tragedy focusing on Iphigenia's sacrifice at Aulis.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A tragedy focusing on Iphigenia as a priestess in Tauris and her reunion with Orestes.
sources.text: A tragedy focusing on Iphigenia as a priestess in Tauris and her reunion with Orestes.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: A collection of myths that includes accounts of Iphigenia's story.
